Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Certify Class
Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Burton of Hersh & Hersh telephone number 415-441-5544 for Plaintiff Daniel Seda.
Jay Conolly and Joseph Orzano of Seyfarth Shaw LLP telephone number 415-397-2823 for Defendant Laseraway Medical Group, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DANIEL SEDA'S Motion To Certify Class is denied without prejudice to being refiled if and when Mr. Seda is able to remedy the deficiencies identified in this order and/or based on a different class definition than what is proposed in the moving papers. Based on the record on this motion, the class definition proposed in the moving papers suffers several serious flaws. First, Mr. Seda has not shown that he is a member of the class he seeks to represent. Second, Mr. Seda has not provided an acceptable plan to identify members of the class and defendants have shown that it is exceedingly difficult, if not impossible, to identify class members solely by reviewing documents. Third, the class definition is based on a legal and factual theory (i.e. LaserAway's telemedicine examinations are illegal) that was not pled in the first amended complaint and thus defendants have been deprived of the opportunity to challenge that theory by way of motion practice. Fourth, it appears that the class definition is overbroad in that it includes class members who have not suffered any harm. Fifth,it appears that the class definition covers people who have signed arbitration agreements and class waivers and the validity of those agreements and waivers has not been adjudicated. Sixth, Mr. Seda provides no useful estimate of the number of people in the class and has not shown that there are numerous class members. Seventh, Mr. Seda has not provided any useful definition of the critical word "unconsented." Eighth, the class defintion is not limited to tattoo removal patients and Mr. Seda has not shown that patients receiving medical services other than tattoo removal are similarly situated to tattoo removal patients and can be adequately represented by a tatoo removal patient. Ninth, Mr. Seda has not presented any plan to take into account the apparently undisputed evidence that prices are negotiated by patients and there are no uniform prices. Tenth, Mr. Seda has not presented any plan to show how reliance on the part of class members on defendants' misrepresenations or omissions can be proved on a class-wide basis. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LASERWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC., WENDY COLE, JEANETTE GUIRAL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action [relief per CCP 1985.7]. That provision merely allows plaintiff to obtain an OSC for the production of medical records. It is not a vehicle to enjoin the illegal practice of medicine. Plaintiff can obtain an injunction on the UCL cause of action. The court strikes the 6th cause of action [unjust enrichment] because the court previously sustained a demurrer without leave to amend as to that claim. The court overrules the remainder of the demurrer. Pars. 30 and 38 of the amended complaint allege that no physician supervised the procedure. That sufficiently alleges illegal conduct and the court liberally reads the amended complaint per CCP 452. The court can resolve class claims on demurrer. In this case, however, defendant raises factual issues, e.g, who treated plaintiff, what type of supervision existed, that would be better addressed on a class certification motion. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
